pending_tickets.thesis: Cleaner, quality expression of the same washout-bounce idea. AI bellwether, 1x (no leverage into the broken regime); only fires on confirmation, so no knife-catch. Mutually-exclusive with BE by buying power.

coverage.name: Bloom Energy
coverage.thesis: **The day's catalyst leader (+6.8%) on a $1.7B fuel-cell-for-AI-datacenter deal.** Energy-for-AI theme, Strong Buy (avg target $281), Q2 earnings 7/28. Live ticket -2 (approve-now ~$220, stop $188) — but it carries FRESH short-seller allegations, so it's the bolder / higher-variance shot vs the cleaner NVDA -3.
coverage.hold_reason: Not held — armed via live ticket -2. Up +6.8% today as the tape sides with the $1.7B deal over short-seller FUD. Honest risk: a follow-up short report could gap it through the $188 stop; the wide stop bounds normal moves, not a gap. Offered alongside the cleaner NVDA -3.
coverage.size_note: Concentrated in the leader; live via one-tap ticket, stop $188.

coverage.name: Rocket Lab
coverage.thesis: **Space/rockets — a strong oversold bouncer (+3.6%).** Snapped back from yesterday's -11.6% flush along with the other deepest-hit names. Not a live vehicle today (I'm using NVDA/BE); watch whether it can build a base above $67 rather than round-trip the bounce.
coverage.hold_reason: Rocket Lab fell 12% yesterday because one analyst went Neutral on its pending $8B Iridium deal, but the deal and its NASA launch wins are very much intact and targets sit $110-130. We bought the reclaim in paper (70 sh @ $69.54) as it made the day's highs - a beaten-down name bouncing on relative strength, not a knife-catch. We ride it toward the $74-80 gap it fell from; the $60 stop is our line.

projection.basis: space momentum leader, +2.8% off the flush

coverage.name: 3x Semis (Direxion)
coverage.thesis: **3x semis — the washout epicenter.** Crashed to $116.58 (-18%) at the open then ripped +20% to $140. Can't add 3x live in a broken regime, but it's the max-torque bounce vehicle; paper has a resting pullback buy at $133 (stop $114) to add on weakness, not chase the midday high.
coverage.hold_reason: SOXL is the 3x semiconductor ETF - the most violent thing on the board, down to capitulation this morning then ripping 19% off the low. A concurrent read added 80 sh in paper (stop $125) and armed a live buy-stop at $143 that only fires if it reclaims green, so it's a confirmation bounce, not a knife-catch. We're honest it's counter-regime (QQQ still under its 20-day), so it stays starter-sized behind a wide stop; a weekend chip or Iran headline is the risk.
coverage.size_note: Counter-regime bounce; live only fires on a $143 reclaim, stop $124-125.

projection.basis: 3x-semi snapback if the SOX reclaim holds

coverage.name: Rigetti
coverage.thesis: **Quantum, +1.1% and green - a speculative bounce off the $13.4 low.** Small, volatile, momentum-only with no fresh catalyst today. Watch, don't chase.
coverage.size_note: Momentum-only; no position, watch for a quantum-sector day.

projection.basis: quantum small-cap bouncing with risk appetite

coverage.name: Constellation Energy
coverage.thesis: **One of the few green names (+0.4%) - nuclear/energy-for-AI, holding up while high-beta tech bleeds.** Held 16 sh in paper (stop $236). A steady relative-strength hold in a risk-off tape, not a mover.
coverage.hold_reason: Held in the paper lab (16 sh @ $260.68, stop $236). Energy-for-AI nuclear name showing relative strength — green today (+0.3%) while the complex bled. Holding while it leads; the $236 stop is the floor.

coverage.name: IonQ

coverage.name: NVIDIA
coverage.thesis: **Buy the AI bellwether on a confirmed washout reversal.** The worst chip month since 2008 was a mechanical leverage unwind, not a fundamentals break — TSMC +34% growth, 'estimates intact, only the multiple broke.' NVDA is the relative-strength tell (-1.1% vs a wrecked complex, coiling under the $206.20 high).
coverage.hold_reason: Not held — armed via live ticket -3 (the cleaner of two bounce tickets). It only fires if NVDA breaks $206.60, confirming the reversal has afternoon legs, so it never knife-catches. We'd ride toward its $215-220 range or exit on the $190.50 stop.
coverage.size_note: Shares held (stop $186); calls cut at -38%. Live: breakout pre-arm $206.60 (ticket -3), stop $190.50.

projection.basis: RS bellwether coiling under the $206.60 breakout

coverage.size_note: No position; watch a clean reclaim as a group tell.

coverage.name: 3x S&P (ProShares)
coverage.thesis: **3x S&P-500, down 1.8% as the broad tape derates (SPY -0.7%).** Held 95 sh in paper (stop $124). Least-bad leveraged long since SPY holds above its MAs (tech-specific selloff), but still leverage in a soft tape.
coverage.hold_reason: UPRO is 3x the S&P - we hold 95 sh in paper (stop $124). It's the most defensible of our leveraged longs because SPY is still above its moving averages (this is a tech-specific selloff, not a broad crash), but it's still leverage in a soft tape, so the stop matters. We'd trim it before adding any new index leverage.

projection.basis: 3x-SPY; SPY holds MAs but broken-Nasdaq drag

coverage.name: Palantir
coverage.thesis: **AI-software — the laggard of the bounce (-2.1%).** Retired its live reclaim-ticket today: it never came near the $134.8 trigger while the rest of the complex bounced. Relative weakness; watch for a real base before re-arming.
coverage.hold_reason: Palantir is a high-beta software name with no news today - it's just bleeding with the risk-off tape. We hold 50 sh in paper (stop $121.5), but it's a laggard, and if $129-130 breaks we'd rotate that capital into something with an actual catalyst. For now the stop does the work.

projection.basis: AI-software laggard testing shelf; no reclaim yet

coverage.size_note: Stopped live; no re-entry until QQQ reclaims ~$718.

coverage.size_note: Stopped live; 3x FANG, avoid until regime heals.

coverage.name: 3x Inverse Semis
coverage.thesis: **Inverse semis — the trade is DONE post-reversal.** Spiked +18% at the open when semis crashed, then collapsed to +2% as they V-bounced. Chasing a fading inverse into a washout-bounce is the wrong side; avoid.
coverage.size_note: Inverse already paid + 2 days extended; don't chase.

feed.text: 12:52pm check-in: reconciled live + paper off the broker — zero naked, nothing new traded. BE (approve-now) + NVDA (breakout pre-arm) still rest for one tap till 4pm.
Also staged the cleaner shot — NVDA breakout over $206.60. Only fires if the bellwether confirms the bounce has afternoon legs; no knife-catch. Mutually exclusive with BE.
Staged BE — the day's biggest watchlist mover (+6.8%) on a real $1.7B fuel-cell-for-AI deal. It's the bold one: fresh short-seller noise makes it higher-variance. Approve = buy now.
Retired the PLTR approve — Adam tapped it at 9:16 but its $134.8 reclaim trigger never hit (PLTR lagged all day, -2.1%), so nothing filled. Aimed the freed cash at the actual leader instead.
FNGU stopped at $25.29 — the open gapped straight through the stop near the low. Bounded the loss but sold the bottom (it's since bounced to ~$26.6). Tuition for carrying 3x into a broken tape.
TQQQ stopped at $66.38 on the gap-down open — de-risked. Then the whole complex V-reversed off the low. The stop did its job; the mistake was upstream (being in 3x here at all).

feed.detail: paper ~82% deployed; dup SOXL add cancelled
feed.text: Paper lab is now ~82% into the bounce leaders — the bleeding NVDA calls got cut and BE/RKLB/SOXL added, all GTC-stopped, zero naked. Cancelled a duplicate SOXL add I'd staged so it wouldn't over-concentrate.

Two live candidates now staged, mutually exclusive (first to fill takes the cash): BE - bold, the day's leader on the $1.7B AI-power deal, but it carries a fresh 7/16 short-seller overhang - and a cleaner NVDA breakout pre-arm ($206.60 buy-stop, 1x not 3x, the RS-bellwether bounce). Both are single names, not 3x-index, so both respect the still-broken QQQ gate.
Staged a live BE buy (one tap) - it's the day's biggest mover on a fresh $1.7B AI-power funding deal. Concentrating our stopped-out cash into the confirmed leader, stop at $188.
Paper grabbed RKLB on the reclaim - it got hit 12% yesterday on one analyst going Neutral, but the Iridium deal and NASA wins are intact and it's bouncing at the day's highs. Stop $60.
Paper went heavy into BE - the $1.7B AI-power funding is the cleanest catalyst on the board and it's leading a red tape. Stop $188.
Cut the NVDA calls in paper at -38%. NVDA's the epicenter of this chip dump and OTM calls decaying in a broken regime are dead money - moved it to what's actually working.
FNGU hit its $25.30 stop at the open - bought it yesterday, the chip+Netflix dump gapped it right through. That's the stop doing its job.
TQQQ stopped at the open too - held it a week, the broken Nasdaq finally took it out. We're fully out of 3x-index now, which is exactly where the regime says to be.

~12:48pm verify-and-hold: both books reconciled clean vs the broker, zero naked. Live flat/cash $869 (both 3x stops filled at the open); paper's 7 legs (BE/RKLB/SOXL + NVDA/PLTR/CEG/UPRO) all GTC-stopped. Regime still broken (QQQ ~$700 < 20-day $719.70) - holding cash, not chasing the V-bounce; re-lever only on a QQQ 20-day reclaim.
TQQQ $68.50 GTC stop fired at the open - QQQ gapped 3% under its 20-day, so the 3x-Nasdaq core sold at $66.38 (-$99 vs the $76.29 entry). The de-risk the plan called for, executed at the gap-low.
FNGU $25.30 GTC stop fired at $25.29 - the 3x-FANG sleeve, dragged by Netflix -10%, closed for -$25. Adding it 7/16 into a rolling-over regime was the mistake; it is now off the book.

Midday: live book is 100% cash ($869) after both stops. Not chasing the junk-led bounce (BE +6.3%, RKLB +3.4%) with the regime still broken - armed a PLTR $134.8 reclaim buy-stop as the 1x redeploy and kept the rest dry. No new leverage until QQQ reclaims its 20-day (~$719).

TQQQ stop hit at the open - 10 sh out at $66.38 (stop $68.50, gapped clean through). Entry was $76.29, so -$99. It's already bounced to $68.85; the stop caught the low. The regime gate did its job - just at a bad tick.
FNGU stopped at $25.29 (-$25 from the $29.43 entry). I bought it 7/16 at the open into a broken regime - that add was the mistake, owning it plainly.
Paper bought BE 40 sh @ $218.90, stop $188 - the one green leader on the board (+6.5%), power-for-AI, on the right side of the ex-semis rotation. The lab's bounce shot.
Paper added RKLB 70 sh @ $69.54, stop $60 - space beta bouncing off deeply oversold with the tape.
Paper took SOXL 80 sh @ $138.75, stop $125 - semis went +20% off the open low; the lab's leveraged bet that the reset is in. Bounded by the stop, and only in paper.
Live 100% cash ($869) and staying there this run - not re-levering or chasing the bounce into a Friday weekend in the worst chip month since 2008. Redeploy trigger: QQQ reclaims its 20-day (~720).

5:42am pre-market: new trading day, plan set. Broker re-verified - live TQQQ 10 (@ $68.50 GTC) + FNGU 6 (@ $25.30 GTC) stopped, paper legs stopped, zero naked. QQQ $705.94 still under its 20-day ($719.70) & 50-day ($718.73) = leverage regime broken; SPY holds above its MAs (tech-specific, not broad). TSMC capex fear + Netflix -8% + Alphabet Gemini delay keep tech heavy. No pre-market action (extended hours is limit-only, no stop). Open plan: trim TQQQ into any bounce, lean on trimming FNGU, no new leverage until QQQ reclaims ~$720.

pulse.text: Midday 7/17: washed out of both 3x-longs at the gap-down open (TQQQ stopped $66.38, FNGU $25.29), so live is flat at ~$869 (-6.7% today - the inherited cost of carrying leverage into a broken regime). QQQ (698.6) is STILL under its 20-day (~718) and 50-day (~715), so I am NOT re-levering 3x-index - that's the exact 4-day mistake. Instead I'm rotating the freed cash into the day's actual catalyst leader: BE +6.6% on a FRESH $1.7B AI-infrastructure funding deal (Oaktree/IDF, fuel-cell data-center power; Strong Buy, target $281, earnings 7/28) - armed as a one-tap live ticket (3 sh, stop $188). Paper did the same: cut the -38% NVDA Aug-$220 calls, bought BE (40 sh) + RKLB (70 sh), the two green leaders. Capture is ~0% again (red while BE ran) - 5th straight, all on the inherited positioning; the structural fix - single-name catalysts over 3x-index beta - is finally in motion. A concurrent engine also armed a SOXL-reclaim buy-stop ($143 trigger); I left it as the alt candidate but BE is my higher-conviction call. Live same-day round-trips used this week: 0 (both exits were multi-day swings, PDT-free). UPDATE: two live candidates now staged as mutually-exclusive alternatives (whichever fills first takes the ~$869) - BE (the bold catalyst-leader tap) and a CLEANER NVDA breakout pre-arm (buy-stop $206.60, 1x not 3x, the RS-bellwether version of the same bounce). Honest flag I under-weighted at first: BE also took a FRESH short-seller report on 7/16 (part of that day's reversal) - an unresolved overhang with weekend gap risk a stop can't fully cover, so BE is the higher-variance play and NVDA is the safer expression of the same thesis.

The tape then ripped a violent oversold reversal (SOXL -18% to -2.3%), but it is junk-led (BE +6.3%, RKLB +3.4%) with quality lagging (NVDA -1.4%, PLTR -2.3%) and the regime still broken - so I am NOT chasing it. Paper ($91.2k, -3.1%) holds its stopped book + the convex NVDA calls, powder dry. PDT budget clean (0 same-day round-trips).

Recomputed QQQ off fresh daily bars: close $705.9 vs 20-day ~$719.7 and 50-day ~$718.7 - still under BOTH, regime stays broken. Nasdaq-100 futures -0.3% with fresh FANG drags (Netflix -8%, Alphabet's Gemini delay) + an oil/Iran overhang - soft open, no bullish catalyst. Acting on last night's lesson: DE-RISK is staged for the open - trim TQQQ into any bounce (multi-day swing, PDT-free), lean on trimming FNGU given the Netflix drag, add NO new leverage until QQQ reclaims its 20-day. Freed cash stays ready to rotate into the strongest CONFIRMED mover (a semi reclaim > SOXL), not a knife-catch. chg_pct shown pre-open = futures-implied, not live prints.
